steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to 'emphasize the size of the sculpture.' Generic words like 'large' or 'monumental' are weak — concrete measurements make scale vivid. The notes give exact numbers: Cycle is roughly 62 feet wide and 56 feet tall.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Adrian Cole is an American artist known for his large metal sculptures.: Uses vague descriptors without dimensions, so the reader gets no real sense of scale. ✗
  • Choice B — Cycle—a large metal sculpture by artist Adrian Cole—stretches roughly 62 feet wide and 56 feet tall.: Supplies the 62-foot and 56-foot figures and uses active language ('stretches') to help the reader feel the size. ✓
  • Choice C — American artist Adrian Cole creates large sculptures from metals such as weathering steel.: Mentions what the sculpture is made of, not how big it is — doesn't address size. ✗
  • Choice D — Adrian Cole's Cycle is made of weathering steel.: Centers on what the work is made of, not its physical scale. ✗
That is the correct answer.
